Question:
During 850 C creep, which factor can control gamma-prime rafting in a directionally solidified nickel-based superalloy?
A. Aluminum mobility
B. Markdown heading depth
C. PDF filename length
D. API retry counter

[{"contents": "During 850 C creep of a directionally solidified nickel-based superalloy, aluminum mobility can control gamma-prime rafting. The longitudinal condition activates more slip systems and APB shearing, while the transverse condition is dominated by fewer slip bands and SISF-related deformation.", "id": "ns_ht_creep_orientation_850c_001"}, {"contents": "Rejuvenation heat treatment can recover creep-degraded directionally solidified superalloys by modifying gamma-prime morphology. In one optimized regime, solution treatment near 1283 C and primary aging near 1095 C restored most standard heat-treatment creep life and increased total creep life.", "id": "ns_ht_rejuvenation_heat_treatment_001"}, {"contents": "Phase-field-informed machine learning can predict creep strain from simulated microstructure and composition descriptors. This process can create precipitate-free zones and grain-boundary damage, with cavitation morphology depending on stress.", "id": "ns_ht_am_abd900_carbide_damage_001"}]
